title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Tue Apr 21, 2026
summary: Israeli settlers killed a 14-year-old student (Aws Hamdi al-Na'san) and a 32-year-old man (Jihad Marzouq Abu Na'im) in an attack on al-Mughayyir Boys School east of Ramallah — one of the deadliest single-day settler incidents of 2026. title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: April 10, 2026 marks six months since the US-brokered Gaza ceasefire took effect, with 738 Palestinians killed since the truce — an average of more than four per day. Israel bombed Gaza on 36 of the last 40 days while waging war on Iran, and the total Palestinian death toll has reached 72,317. Only 21% of promised aid trucks have entered Gaza, causing dramatic food price increases. Al Jazeera described the situation as 'neither war nor peace.' The UN Commission of Inquiry warned that the Iran war has eclipsed a surge in Palestinian human rights violations, condemning Israel's new death penalty law. Phase 2 ceasefire talks remain deadlocked.

title: Breaking: Thousands Pray at Al-Aqsa as Israel Lifts 40-Day Access Ban
summary: Thousands of Palestinians returned to Al-Aqsa Mosque on April 9, 2026 after Israeli authorities lifted a 40-day ban on Muslim worshippers' access to Islam's third holiest site in Jerusalem.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: The UN Security Council held an open debate on April 8, 2026 on consolidating the Gaza ceasefire and advancing Phase 2 of the Trump administration's 20-point plan, with Deputy Special Coordinator Alakbarov warning that Israel is withholding $2.5 billion in Palestinian clearance revenue. The WHO kept medical evacuations from Gaza suspended following the killing of contractor Majdi Aslan by Israeli forces on April 6, leaving 18,000+ patients awaiting evacuation. Israeli airstrikes on April 7 killed at least 10 Palestinians at Maghazi refugee camp after an Israeli-backed militia attempted to abduct Hamas operatives. The April 7 event file was also updated to include the WHO evacuation suspension announcement and Maghazi strikes, previously missed.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Apr 3, 2026
summary: Day 35 of the US-Israel war on Iran saw strikes on Iran's century-old Pasteur Institute, a military-logistics bridge, and steel plants, with the cumulative Iranian death toll reaching 2,076. Gaza ceasefire Phase 2 negotiations remain completely stalled — 689+ Palestinians killed since the October 2025 ceasefire — as the Iran war has frozen Trump administration Gaza diplomacy entirely; food price inflation in Gaza hit 305% and 1.6 million Gazans face acute food insecurity. IDF Chief Zamir suspended a reserve battalion after it assaulted and detained a CNN news crew in the West Bank, while warning the IDF could 'collapse on itself' across five simultaneous fronts. Operation Iron Wall in Jenin and Tulkarm continues with 40,000+ displaced; 257 settler violence incidents were recorded in the first 25 days of the Iran war.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Apr 2, 2026
summary: Iran launched its largest missile barrage in three weeks on Passover (April 1, Day 33), firing ~10 ballistic missiles at Israel — most reportedly carrying cluster munitions — with one striking Bnei Brak and wounding 10 people including a girl in critical condition. IDF strikes killed 9 Palestinians across Gaza on April 1, including Hamas anti-tank corps commander Kamal Ayash killed in Al-Mawasi. Palestinians across the West Bank held a general strike against Israel's new death penalty law (passed March 30); IDF killed 2 in a rock-throwing incident, raising the West Bank toll to 1,138+. Trump claimed Iran's president requested a ceasefire on Truth Social — Iran immediately denied it — and reversed course by demanding Hormuz reopening as a precondition, while Houthis launched a coordinated barrage with Iran and Hezbollah.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: Iran Launches Biggest Missile Barrage in Three Weeks on Passover; Bnei Brak Struck, 10 Injured. West Bank Observes General Strike Against Israel's New Death Penalty Law; IDF Kills 2 in Rock-Throwing Incident. Trump Claims Iran Requested Ceasefire; Iran Denies; Trump Demands Hormuz Reopening as Precondition.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Mar 31, 2026
summary: Israeli strikes killed 3 Palestinians in Gaza City's Zeitoun neighborhood on March 30 and 5 more in two attacks on March 31, with a further 3 Palestinians killed in the West Bank on Palestine Land Day's 50th anniversary — including a 15-year-old at Dheisheh refugee camp near Bethlehem. Iran launched another ballistic missile barrage on Day 31 (March 30) wounding 10 Israelis, as Human Rights Watch confirmed Iran had used cluster munitions against Israeli population centers including Yehud and Ramat Gan. The Wall Street Journal reported Trump told aides on March 31 he is willing to end the Iran war without requiring the Strait of Hormuz to reopen, while France denied Israeli use of French airspace for US weapons and Italy blocked US military aircraft at Sicily — marking the deepest NATO fracture since the war began. A CNN news team was also assaulted and detained by Israeli soldiers in the West Bank on March 31.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Israeli airstrikes killed at least three Palestinians and wounded two others in Gaza City's Zeitoun neighborhood on March 30 as the IDF stated it had targeted a Hamas armed cell. Iranian ballistic missiles struck Israel on March 30 — Day 31 of the US-Israel war on Iran — wounding at least 10 Israelis. Palestine Land Day 50th Anniversary Marked Amid Escalating Settler Violence and Iranian Missile War.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: An Iranian ballistic missile struck the Ne'ot Hovav chemical industrial zone south of Beersheba on March 29, triggering hazmat protocols — the second Iranian air defense penetration in two days after the Eshtaol strike on March 28. IDF airstrikes killed at least six Palestinians including a child at police checkpoints in southern Khan Younis, with two brothers also killed in a separate Shejaiya strike; Gaza's death toll since October 7 now stands at 72,267+ documented (691 since the ceasefire). Israel barred the Latin Patriarch from Palm Sunday Mass at the Church of the Holy Sepulchre for the first time in modern history, drawing EU condemnation from foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas as a 'violation of religious freedom.' The three-front Axis of Resistance assault — Iran missiles, Houthi second wave, and Hezbollah rockets at Tiberias — continued as Day 30 of the US-Israel war on Iran closed with 1,900+ Iranians killed since February 28.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Iran launched six separate ballistic missile salvos at Israel on March 29 as the U.S.-Israel war entered its second month, triggering sirens across central Israel and the West Bank with an impact near Beersheba. Yemen's Houthis launched a second wave of missiles and drones within 24 hours of re-entering the conflict on March 28, with all projectiles intercepted. Hezbollah fired rockets at Tiberias and northern Israeli towns in a coordinated multi-front Axis of Resistance assault designed to strain Israeli air defense capacity simultaneously. The simultaneous three-front attack — Iran from the east, Houthis from the south, Hezbollah from the north — marks the most operationally coordinated strike against Israel since the conflict began, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining closed and global oil markets disrupted.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Mar 28, 2026
summary: Yemen's Houthis entered the Iran-Israel war on March 28 with their first ballistic missile attack on Israel since their November 2025 ceasefire freeze, firing toward the Negev and citing solidarity with Iran, Lebanon, and Palestine. An Iranian ballistic missile separately struck the Jerusalem-area town of Eshtaol, injuring 11 people and evading air defense — prompting an IAF investigation. In Gaza, IDF drone and airstrikes killed at least three people including Hamas-affiliated operatives near Khan Younis and in Shejaiya, with the IDF citing ongoing ceasefire violations. The UK hosted the inaugural International Peace Fund for Israeli-Palestinian Peace conference at Lancaster House, with the Palestinian embassy in London officially opening following UK recognition of Palestine in September 2025. COGAT's suspension of all UNDP shipments over an engine oil smuggling incident continues to compound Gaza's humanitarian bottleneck at Kerem Shalom.

title: Israel-Palestine Conflict Tracker Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: Israeli settlers shot and killed Palestinian-American teenager Nasrallah Abu Siyam in the West Bank village of Mukhmas — the second Palestinian-American killed by settlers in under a year. Details of the Board of Peace eight-month disarmament plan emerged, requiring full Hamas disarmament by Day 90 and conditioning reconstruction on compliance; Hamas immediately rejected it. IDF Chief of Staff Zamir warned the security cabinet of imminent force collapse across five simultaneous fronts, and 15 diplomatic missions condemned 'settler terror' as 257 incidents were recorded in 25 days since February 28. Gaza's documented death toll stands at 72,253 per UNRWA; food price inflation reached 305% in March as aid trucks remain 80% below pre-Iran-war levels.

title: Israel-Palestine Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Israeli settler violence surged dramatically across the West Bank during Eid al-Fitr, with approximately 100 masked settlers torching homes, vehicles, and a village council building in Jalud and Qaryut on March 22, with attacks continuing through a second night. In an unprecedented move, Israel barred Muslim worshippers from the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound on Eid for the first time since 1967, deploying stun grenades and tear gas against crowds attempting to pray outside the Old City gates. Gaza's humanitarian situation remains critical with all crossings except the partially operational Kerem Shalom closed since February 28, leaving 46% of essential medicines and 66% of medical consumables out of stock. The UN Human Rights Office published a major report on March 17 warning that Israel's accelerating settlement expansion — including 1,732 documented settler violence incidents — is driving mass forced displacement across the West Bank, raising concerns of ethnic cleansing. Progress on the Trump 20-point peace plan has stalled, the Palestinian Authority faces financial collapse as Israel withholds $2.5 billion in clearance revenue, and the ICC warrants against Netanyahu and Gallant remain active.
